refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, measurement/telemetry, operator interface (local or remote), and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome (what must be achieved) from the mechanisms (how it is achieved). The refined FR explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) and embeds safety and policy compliance as non-negotiable constraints.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials over the agreed evaluation period; measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) against baseline and target; cross-check against worksite safety plan compliance logs and operational policy audit trails.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time and payload capacity sufficient to meet productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time telemetry (position, load, cycle state, fuel, health) to enable operator decision-making and remote monitoring.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (geofence, speed limits, load limits, emergency stop) and alert operators to safety-critical conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator to command and monitor the vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity and safety.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-payload, fuel-efficient vehicle with optimized hydraulic and engine systems; equip with onboard telemetry sensors; implement local operator cabin with intuitive controls; add remote teleoperation capability via secure network link.

DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with swappable powertrains (diesel, electric, hybrid); implement distributed sensor architecture with edge computing for real-time telemetry; provide dual-mode operator interface (cabin + remote); integrate safety services as a centralized policy enforcement layer.

DP-C: Procure a baseline commercial earthmoving vehicle; retrofit with aftermarket telemetry and remote control modules; implement safety constraints via software overlay on vehicle CAN bus; provide operator interface via tablet/web application.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with swappable powertrains (diesel, electric, hybrid); implement distributed sensor architecture with edge computing for real-time telemetry; provide dual-mode operator interface (cabin + remote); integrate safety services as a centralized policy enforcement layer.

potential_sources: Telemetry latency and fidelity depend on network link quality and edge computing responsiveness; must be co-designed with Network Link interface.
Safety constraint enforcement (geofence, speed, load limits) couples teleoperation command validation with Safety Services; requires clear handoff of authority between local and remote operators.
Powertrain modularity couples vehicle mechanical design with fuel/energy management telemetry; must ensure sensor coverage across all powertrain variants.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B exhibits controlled coupling through explicit interface definitions (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). The modular powertrain approach reduces coupling to specific engine/hydraulic implementations while maintaining productivity measurement consistency. Safety services as a centralized layer decouples operator intent from safety enforcement, reducing risk of conflicting commands.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Edge computing on vehicle is reliable and maintainable in field conditions (dust, vibration, temperature extremes).
ASSUMPTION: Network link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for real-time telemetry and remote tasking.
ASSUMPTION: Powertrain modularity does not degrade cycle time or payload capacity below productivity_target.
RISK: Distributed sensor architecture increases complexity and potential failure modes; mitigation requires robust sensor health monitoring and graceful degradation.
RISK: Dual-mode operator interface (cabin + remote) may create confusion or safety gaps if mode switching is not explicit and auditable.
RISK: Safety services as a centralized policy layer may become a bottleneck if policy updates are frequent or context-dependent.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the decision logic for mode switching (local ↔ remote) and the authority hierarchy during transitions. Define minimum acceptable telemetry latency and fidelity for safe remote operation. Specify powertrain variants and their impact on productivity baseline.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration boundary encompassing the Vehicle, Teleoperation subsystem, Safety Services, and all inter-module interfaces. It is responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery, safety compliance, and operator experience across both on-site and remote modes.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time vehicle state (position, load, cycle state, fuel, health, powertrain status) streamed from Vehicle and Safety Services to external monitoring systems (Site Manager dashboard, Maintenance systems).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands (move, dig, load, dump, emergency stop) received from on-site operator cabin or remote tele-operator interface; routed through Safety Services for constraint validation before execution by Vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical alerts (geofence breach, speed limit exceeded, load limit exceeded, emergency stop triggered, sensor failure) generated by Safety Services and transmitted to operators and Site Manager.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Secure, low-latency communication channel between on-site Vehicle/Teleoperation and remote tele-operator; carries Tasking commands (in) and Telemetry (out); subject to availability and latency SLAs.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the designated top-level integration boundary. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within this module. No diffusion of productivity logic across external modules.

Vehicle module owns the mechanical, hydraulic, and powertrain systems that directly execute earthmoving tasks and generate telemetry. However, productivity measurement and safety constraint enforcement are diffused to Safety Services and Teleoperation modules. Vehicle is a sub-module of NGHE Solution.

Teleoperation module owns the remote operator interface and command transmission logic. However, command validation and safety enforcement are delegated to Safety Services; vehicle control execution is delegated to Vehicle. Teleoperation is a sub-module of NGHE Solution.

Safety Services owns policy enforcement (geofence, speed, load limits), alert generation, and mode switching logic. However, actual vehicle control and telemetry generation are owned by Vehicle and Teleoperation. Safety Services is a sub-module of NGHE Solution and acts as a cross-cutting policy layer.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (top-level integration owner); with sub-ownership delegated to Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (remote operator interface), and Safety Services (policy enforcement).
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Productivity delivery is best contained within the NGHE Solution boundary because it requires orchestration of Vehicle execution, Teleoperation command routing, Safety Services constraint enforcement, and Telemetry feedback. No single sub-module can independently achieve productivity_target; the integration of all four is necessary. This containment avoids diffusion of productivity logic across external systems.
containment_risks: RISK: If Vehicle powertrain variants are not fully characterized, productivity baseline may vary unpredictably; mitigation: establish productivity baseline for each powertrain variant before field trials.
RISK: If Network Link latency exceeds acceptable threshold, remote teleoperation may become unsafe or ineffective; mitigation: define latency SLA and implement fallback to local-only operation.

RISK: If Safety Services policy updates are frequent or context-dependent (e.g., different policies for different worksites), the centralized policy layer may become a bottleneck; mitigation: implement policy versioning and site-specific configuration management.
RISK: If Teleoperation and on-site operator interfaces are not synchronized, mode switching may create safety gaps; mitigation: implement explicit mode handoff protocol with audit trail.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear ownership boundaries and interface contracts between NGHE Solution and its sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Establish SLAs for Network Link latency, Telemetry fidelity, and SafetyAlert response time. Create a configuration management plan for Safety Services policies across different worksites.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (modular vehicle platform with distributed telemetry, dual-mode operator interface, and centralized safety services) directly addresses the refined FR by enabling task execution with measurable productivity, real-time telemetry, safety constraint enforcement, and remote operation capability. The DP is logically sound and does not introduce unnecessary complexity. Coupling is controlled through explicit interface definitions.

diffusion_risks: Productivity measurement logic is distributed across Vehicle (cycle time, payload), Teleoperation (command latency), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement); mitigation: establish a unified productivity KPI calculation module within NGHE Solution that aggregates data from all three sub-modules.
Telemetry generation is distributed across Vehicle sensors and Safety Services alerts; mitigation: define a common telemetry schema and ensure all sources conform to it.

Safety constraint enforcement is centralized in Safety Services, but command validation may also occur in Teleoperation and Vehicle; mitigation: establish a clear hierarchy of validation (Teleoperation → Safety Services → Vehicle) to avoid redundant or conflicting checks.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate top-level IM boundary because it integrates all sub-modules and interfaces. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that contribute to productivity delivery but do not independently achieve it. The four known interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are all contained within or cross the NGHE Solution boundary, confirming proper architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Productivity can be verified through field trials measuring m³/h or ton/h against baseline and target. Safety compliance can be verified through audit trails of SafetyAlert events and policy enforcement logs. Operator experience can be verified through usability testing of both on-site and remote interfaces. All verification methods are feasible within the NGHE Solution boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The refined FR is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution with sub-allocations to Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (remote operation), and Safety Services (safety compliance). Each sub-module has a clear responsibility and measurable contribution to the overall productivity target.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit SLAs for Network Link latency, Telemetry fidelity, and SafetyAlert response time to ensure remote operation is safe and effective.
Establish a productivity baseline for each powertrain variant (diesel, electric, hybrid) to account for differences in cycle time and fuel efficiency.
Create a mode switching protocol that clearly defines authority transitions between on-site and remote operators, with audit trail and safety interlocks.
Implement a unified productivity KPI calculation module within NGHE Solution that aggregates data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

decision.justification: The refined FR is not atomic; it contains four distinct sub-FRs (task execution, telemetry, safety, remote operation) that require further decomposition. Each sub-FR maps to a different sub-module (Vehicle, Telemetry/Teleoperation, Safety Services) and has distinct verification criteria. Proceeding to the next level of decomposition will clarify the design intent, reduce coupling, and enable parallel development of sub-modules.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time and payload capacity sufficient to meet productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time telemetry (position, load, cycle state, fuel, health) to enable operator decision-making and remote monitoring.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (geofence, speed limits, load limits, emergency stop) and alert operators to safety-critical conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator to command and monitor the vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity and safety.

assumptions: Productivity_target is defined by customer and is achievable with current earthmoving technology.
Network Link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for real-time telemetry and remote tasking.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and can be encoded as constraints in Safety Services.
Powertrain modularity (diesel, electric, hybrid) does not degrade cycle time or payload capacity below productivity_target.
Edge computing on vehicle is reliable and maintainable in field conditions.
Dual-mode operator interface (cabin + remote) can be safely switched without creating safety gaps.
open_risks: Network Link latency may exceed acceptable threshold for safe remote operation; mitigation: define latency SLA and implement fallback to local-only operation.
Powertrain variants may have different productivity baselines; mitigation: establish baseline for each variant before field trials.
Safety Services policy updates may become a bottleneck if frequent or context-dependent; mitigation: implement policy versioning and site-specific configuration.
Mode switching between on-site and remote operators may create safety gaps; mitigation: implement explicit mode handoff protocol with audit trail.
Distributed sensor architecture increases complexity and failure modes; mitigation: implement robust sensor health monitoring and graceful degradation.
evidence_needed: Customer-defined productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) and evaluation period.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies (geofence, speed limits, load limits, emergency procedures).
Powertrain variant specifications and their impact on cycle time and payload capacity.
Operator interface mockups and usability test results for both on-site and remote modes.
Safety Services policy enforcement rules and alert thresholds.
Field trial plan and success criteria for productivity measurement.
